description: The wisdom found in mending what is broken, whether objects, relationships, or systems. It honors the history of damage while creating new strength at the mended places, embodying the philosophy that repair can make things more beautiful than before.
rationale: The word 'lusho' begins with the liquid 'lu', suggesting the flow and flexibility needed in repair work. The final syllable 'sho' uses the soft fricative to represent the gentle, patient craft of mending. Together they create a word that feels like the careful restoration it describes - fluid yet deliberate.
